text.title: Instagram Ads Performance and Reporting
text.summary: Bid strategies, budget scaling rules, and placement-level reporting API for Instagram campaigns.

list.item: Lowest Cost (LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP) — maximize conversions; CPA unstable at scale
Cost Cap — set target CPA ceiling; delivery slows when cost exceeds cap
Bid Cap — controls auction competition; use when CPM inflation is detected

rule.statement: Start with Lowest Cost; switch to Cost Cap only after 50+ conversions per ad set to give the algorithm enough learning signal.
rule.rationale: Cost Cap with insufficient conversion data causes severe under-delivery — the algorithm cannot find users at the target cost if it has no baseline.

rule.statement: Increase budget by 20-30% maximum every 3-4 days; do not double a budget in a single step.
rule.rationale: Large budget jumps force the campaign back into the learning phase, resetting optimization and causing temporary CPA spikes of 40-80%.

example.code: curl -X GET "https://graph.facebook.com/v20.0/{adset-id}/insights?\ fields=spend,impressions,reach,clicks,cpc,cpm,ctr,\ actions,cost_per_action_type,\ video_p25_watched_actions,video_p75_watched_actions,\ video_p100_watched_actions&\ date_preset=last_7d&\ breakdowns=publisher_platform,platform_position&\ access_token={token}"
example.note: breakdowns=platform_position returns rows for instagram_stream, instagram_story, instagram_explore, instagram_reels separately.

rule.statement: Always break down reporting by platform_position when running multi-placement ad sets; never rely on campaign-level aggregates to make placement budget decisions.
rule.rationale: Aggregate metrics hide placement-level CPA variance of 3-5x; scaling on aggregates can double spend on the worst-performing placement.

rule.statement: Monitor frequency and pause or rotate creative when it exceeds 3.0 for any single placement.
rule.rationale: Above frequency 3.0, engagement rates decline and CPAs rise due to audience fatigue — users have seen the ad enough to develop ad blindness.
